message:
Add SQL processing layer crate stubs: ast, parser, planner, vdbe, func

Five crates forming the SQL compilation and execution pipeline, sitting
above the storage layer and below the integration facade.

fsqlite-ast (Abstract Syntax Tree):
- Will define AST node types for all SQL statements (SELECT, INSERT,
  UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE TABLE, etc.) and expressions
- Deps: fsqlite-types
- Pure data structures, no parsing logic

fsqlite-parser (SQL Parser):
- Will implement a hand-written recursive descent parser producing
  the AST types — no parser generators, full control over error messages
- Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-ast, memchr
- Uses memchr for efficient keyword and delimiter scanning in the tokenizer

fsqlite-planner (Query Planner):
- Will handle name resolution, WHERE clause analysis, join ordering,
  index selection, and cost estimation
- Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-ast
- Transforms AST into an optimized logical plan

fsqlite-vdbe (Virtual Database Engine):
- Will implement the bytecode interpreter executing the 190+ opcodes
  defined in fsqlite-types::Opcode against the B-tree storage layer
- Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-pager, fsqlite-btree
- The core execution engine: compiles plans to bytecode, then interprets

fsqlite-func (Built-in Functions):
- Will implement SQLite's scalar functions (abs, length, substr, etc.),
  aggregate functions (count, sum, avg, group_concat), and window
  functions (row_number, rank, dense_rank, ntile, etc.)
- Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
